programmes.show_description: In her own words, Diana Ross tells the story of her life and career in music, from Motown and the Supremes to playing Billie Holliday and her many solo hits.

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: As the clocks go forward, Vernon selects upbeat, uplifting, sing-a-long dance tunes to get you excited for British Summer Time! Plus, there's a Basement Stexx track from Hard Drive.

programmes.show_name: The Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Angela Griffin invites you to explore the natural world with The Wild About Nature Unwinds Mix, featuring Seal, Tom Petty and Taylor Swift.
The Kane brothers, Pat and Greg, performing live at Edinburgh's Playhouse Theatre in 1989. Featuring Labour Of Love, Ordinary Angel, Violently, Looking For Linda and Remote.
Musician, DJ, the 'M' in M People and Mancuniuan music legend Mike Pickering takes us on a ten-song journey through the songs that have soundtracked his life.
Jason Mohammad marks Palm Sunday with Pause For Thought from Father Brian D'Arcy.
Your stories, dedications and classic love songs with Michael... easy like Sunday morning.
Paddy is live from Salford, asking you about the things you've never tried before... Whether it's food, an adventure sport or even a social media.
A celebration of musical theatre hosted by Michelle Visage, packed full of showstoppers and special guest appearances direct from the AO Arena in Manchester.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 70s with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: More great tracks from the 70s. Also on the show, more tracks from Bob's 'Rock Date Diary' plus another 70s Country Classic pick.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1968.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1978.
Tony Blackburn spins his personal pick of the hits from across the decades. To listen on most smart speakers just 'ask BBC Sounds to play Tony Blackburn's Golden Hour'.
Musical Knights including Elton, Rod & Barry. Paul celebrates The Most Important Meal of the Day! And we have a live track from Marvin Gaye.
Angela invites you to switch off and mellow out with tunes from Jorja Smith, Lemar, Roberta Flack and more.

programmes.show_name: Radio 2 Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Escape the grey skies with a playlist made for sunny days, featuring Lenny Kravitz, Everything But The Girl and Spice Girls.
Richie Anderson is live from Salford with your Monday Motivation to kick-start a brand new week, plus Secret Star and This Week in Songs.

programmes.show_name: Alternative Sounds of the 90s with Dermot O'Leary
programmes.show_description: Damian and Timothy from OK Go! pick their favourite 90s artists - Beck and Elliott Smith. Plus, tunes from Pixies, The Charlatans and The Sugarcubes.
Welcome! It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Airdrum Anthems.
Gary's in for Scott to get your morning sorted... with Noah Wyle chatting about The Pitt!
Musician, DJ and the 'M' in M People and Mancuniuan music legend Mike Pickering chooses his Tracks Of My Years. Plus, there's Ten To The Top at 1030 and more Vernon's Vault.
Jeremy and guests discuss the news headlines and talk to the people making them.
Trevor is bringing you some of the best music from across the decades to get you through the afternoon.
Sara gets you through teatime with giggles and some fab music. Plus, she reveals the phrase for Totally Teavoted and a listener plays Jukebox Jam.
Sara plays the biggest and best tunes to get you up dancing!
Jo brings her Shiny Happy Playlist, 30 minutes of feel-good music from 7-7.30pm to soundtrack listeners' weekday evening.
Jo handpicks her favourite new music from all genres of the musical spectrum and quality vintage songs too.

programmes.show_name: The Blues Show with Cerys Matthews
programmes.show_description: British blues and roots artist Sunjay stops by the studio to share his favourite blues records and influences, as well as share details of his forthcoming tenth album.

programmes.show_name: The Good Groove with DJ Spoony
programmes.show_description: Feel-good tunes to make you groove. 'Dr. Beat' is also on hand to prescribe a perfect tune for a listener whose week hasn't got off to the best start!
